summary: Open draft matching PR for game-mode debug results code. The slice replaces the gm_801B0DD0 placeholder in src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c with a matched implementation and refines struct DebugResultsData in gm_1B03.static.h from a large pad to typed bitfields, byte fields, padding, and a MatchEnd member. The PR body reports gm_801B0DD0 as 100% matched, and the decomp-dev bot report confirms one new match in main/melee/gm/gm_1B03 worth +332 bytes.

key_files.role: Replaces /// #gm_801B0DD0 with a C implementation that loads debug results data, builds MatchEnd player standings, computes character result SFX bits, calls audio setup, and uses PAD_STACK(8).
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report one new match gm_801B0DD0 +332 0.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Refines DebugResultsData layout from pad_x0[0x2284] into named bitfields/bytes, pad_x5[3], and MatchEnd match_end used by gm_801B0DD0.

what_changed.text: gm_801B0DD0 was decompiled from a placeholder into a full function taking MinorScene* arg0.

The function obtains DebugResultsData through gm_801A427C(arg0), uses data->match_end as a MatchEnd*, copies fields from un_803FA258 indices 0x5A through 0x60 and 0x51 through 0x59, and calls gm_80166A98 to rebuild match_end standings.
The implementation loops over four player standings and ORs lbAudioAx_80026E84(character_kind) into a u64 sfx_result for non-slot_type-3, non-big-loser players.
The implementation calls lbAudioAx_80026F2C(0x14), lbAudioAx_8002702C(0x4, sfx_result), gm_80168FC4(), gm_801701A0(), and ends with PAD_STACK(8).
DebugResultsData layout was narrowed from a single byte pad of size 0x2284 to explicit x0 bitfields, x1-x4 byte fields, three bytes of padding, and a MatchEnd match_end member.

smart_moves.text: Recovered just enough of DebugResultsData to express gm_801B0DD0 through typed fields and a MatchEnd member instead of keeping all accesses behind a large opaque pad.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:DebugResultsData typed fields and MatchEnd match_end

smart_moves.text: Used a natural four-iteration for loop over player_standings rather than repeated generated blocks for each player.
Kept the matching-only stack tactic narrow: PAD_STACK appears as PAD_STACK(8) at the end of the single newly matched function.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For game-mode debug result code, a partial local struct recovery can be enough to turn an opaque buffer into reviewable source: expose fields touched by the function and preserve unknown bytes as padding.
When a function processes fixed-size player data, test a natural indexed loop over player_standings before preserving unrolled player-specific blocks.

naming_conventions.name: match_end
naming_conventions.lesson: Use a clear local role name for a MatchEnd pointer/member once the type is evident.

naming_conventions.name: sfx_result
naming_conventions.lesson: A local u64 accumulating result fanfare bits was named for its audio result role.

naming_conventions.name: x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, x1, x2, x3, x4, pad_x5
naming_conventions.lesson: For newly recovered but semantically unclear fields, address/offset-style names and explicit padding avoid inventing meaning while improving type structure.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK(8) narrowly in the newly matched function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not include objdiff output beyond the bot report; curator should not generalize PAD_STACK use outside this function.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce bitfields for the first byte of DebugResultsData to match individual assignments to x0_0 and x0_1 while retaining x0_other for the remaining bits.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:u8 x0_0 : 1; u8 x0_1 : 1; u8 x0_other : 6;
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice shows the resulting source and match report, but not the failed alternatives that led to this bitfield shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Cast cached debug-menu values from un_803FA258 to s8 for player identifiers and subtract 1 from several character/slot-like values before passing them to gm_80166A98.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:gm_80166A98 arguments with (s8) un_803FA258[...] and un_803FA258[...] - 1
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The exact semantics of each un_803FA258 slot are not established in this slice.

follow_up_queries: un_803FA258 debug results sound test gm_80166A98

accepted_candidate_records.title: Partial struct recovery can unlock a focused function match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In gm_801B0DD0, replacing a large opaque DebugResultsData pad with only the fields used by the function plus explicit padding and a typed MatchEnd member produced reviewable typed accesses and coincided with a bot-confirmed 100% match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report one new match

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fixed four-player standing scans can match as a natural indexed loop
accepted_candidate_records.text: gm_801B0DD0 uses for (i = 0; i < 4; i++) over match_end->player_standings[i] to accumulate character result SFX bits for eligible players.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:for loop over four player_standings entries

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1B03 debug results screen role
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2489 describes gm_1B03.c as debug results screen code that loads cached match parameters from the sound test/debug menu into the results scene, rebuilds four-player standings, and plays character-specific result victory fanfare. The diff for gm_801B0DD0 supports parts of this by reading un_803FA258, rebuilding MatchEnd via gm_80166A98, iterating player_standings, and calling lbAudioAx result audio functions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact, but should be curator-reviewed because the functional description is from the PR body and includes a generated Claude Code footer.

source_update_candidates.title: Keep PAD_STACK evidence narrow
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2489 is a concrete example where a single PAD_STACK(8) appears in a bot-confirmed matched function. This supports the existing standard that PAD_STACK is a narrow matching tactic requiring evidence, not a general style.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as an example under the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard; not a new standalone rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the 107 failed-function list as lessons; the slice gives little or no evidence beyond names and sparse percentages.
Do not infer that PR #2489 was merged or accepted; loaded metadata says OPEN with merged_at null.
Do not infer reviewer feedback or requested changes; no review comments are loaded.
Do not generalize the specific un_803FA258 offsets into named data semantics without additional source or data evidence.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(8) as generally acceptable outside this narrow matched gm_801B0DD0 case.
